yaml.title: Psychedelic Neuroplasticity & Pancreatic Cancer
yaml.description: Psychedelic Neuroplasticity and the Perineural Microenvironment of Pancreatic Cancer

markdown.markdown:
This project began with a question that, at first glance, seemed to connect two completely unrelated fields.

Psilocybin—the psychoactive compound currently undergoing Phase III clinical trials for treatment-resistant depression—is increasingly being explored as supportive therapy for patients experiencing psychological distress during cancer treatment. Recent work has shown that one of psilocybin's mechanisms of action involves activation of the neurotrophic receptor TrkB, a key regulator of synaptic plasticity and neuronal survival.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), however, is one of the most aggressive malignancies known and displays extensive perineural invasion, a process driven largely through signalling by TrkA, the closest structural and functional homologue of TrkB.

These receptors belong to the same neurotrophin family, share approximately 60% sequence homology, and activate many of the same downstream signalling cascades—including MAPK/ERK and PI3K-Akt.

Yet remarkably, virtually no work has investigated whether these pathways converge within pancreatic tumours themselves.

This observation motivated a computational investigation using publicly available transcriptomic data from The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA).

Rather than asking whether psilocybin causes cancer progression, the project asks a far more fundamental biological question:

Do the molecular pathways through which psilocybin exerts its neuroplastic effects already exist within pancreatic tumours?

## Research Objectives
Characterise expression of NTRK1 (TrkA) and NTRK2 (TrkB) across pancreatic tumours
Determine whether both receptors are co-expressed within individual patients
Identify genes co-regulated with TrkB
Investigate enriched biological pathways associated with TrkB signalling
Explore the biological plausibility of receptor convergence within pancreatic cancer

## Computational Workflow
TCGA-PAAD RNA-seq dataset
UCSC Xena Browser
R
Spearman correlation analysis
Kaplan-Meier survival analysis
Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA)
KEGG pathway enrichment
Publication-quality visualisation

## Key Findings

Analysis of 182 pancreatic tumour samples demonstrated that both TrkA and TrkB are broadly expressed across PDAC.

Expression of the two receptors showed statistically significant positive co-expression, suggesting coordinated regulation within the tumour microenvironment.

Genes most strongly associated with TrkB expression demonstrated enrichment in pathways central to tumour progression, including:

MAPK signalling
PI3K-Akt signalling
Focal adhesion
Cell migration pathways

These findings provide computational evidence supporting further experimental investigation into neurotrophin signalling within pancreatic cancer.
